chunk_summary: Family-supported outpatient management for adolescent eating disorders emphasizes structured monitoring, family-based treatment, and clear escalation rules to ensure medical stability and weight restoration.
learning_objectives: Understand the principles of family-based treatment for adolescents with eating disorders.
Identify medical and behavioral failure signals requiring escalation in outpatient care.
Learn the importance of structured meal plans and regular follow-up intervals in early-phase restriction.
Recognize the role of multidisciplinary teams and telehealth in resource-limited settings.
key_facts: Family-based treatment is first-line for adolescents with anorexia nervosa, focusing on supervised meals and weight restoration.
Weekly reviews are recommended during early-phase restriction until weight gain and resolution of orthostatic symptoms are achieved.
Escalation is required for failure signals such as continued weight loss, syncope, worsening orthostasis, or suicidal ideation.
Baseline medical evaluation includes CBC, electrolytes, glucose, LFT, and ECG when indicated.
Structured meal plans should include three meals and snacks daily, with competitive sports and excessive exercise paused until stabilization.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Family-based treatment principles are first-line for adolescents with anorexia nervosa: parents/caregivers supervise meals and snacks, reduce negotiation around intake, and prioritize weight restoration and medical stability.
document_key_topics: Family-supported outpatient management for eating disorders
Escalation rules for medical and behavioral deterioration
Structured monitoring and meal planning in adolescents
